title: [Bug]: codexPlugins activation stalls every embedded-Codex turn — plugin/list timed out / LOCAL_REQUEST_CANCELLED; curated marketplace + plugin/install contract drift vs Codex 0.143+
author: Kenmege
body:
## Summary

With native `codexPlugins` enabled (curated plugin set configured in `openclaw.json`), **every embedded-Codex agent turn fails** with `plugin/list timed out` (`CODEX_APP_SERVER_LOCAL_REQUEST_CANCELLED`) after ~2 minutes per model attempt, so runs exhaust the whole failover chain and the agent never replies. The Codex app-server itself is healthy — driven directly over stdio JSON-RPC it answers every `plugin/list` shape OpenClaw sends in under 1.1 s.

Root cause appears to be OpenClaw's plugin thread-config/activation path drifting from the current Codex CLI plugin API on two axes (details below). Disabling `codexPlugins` instantly restores turns.

Sanitized from a production gateway; no tokens, hostnames, or account details included.

## Environment

- OpenClaw `2026.7.2` (verified at heads `f0d10afc77b`, `f57645807ff`, and `1a81644bf1f` — see timeline)
- Codex CLI (vendored `@openai/codex`): reproduced on **0.144.1 and 0.144.4**
- macOS 26.5 (arm64), gateway via launchd, default model `openai/gpt-5.5`, fallback `openai/gpt-5.4` (both through the embedded Codex runtime)
- `plugins.entries.codex.config.codexPlugins`: `enabled: true` + 23 curated plugins, each `marketplaceName: "openai-curated"` (e.g. `gmail`, `notion`, `slack`, `figma`, `linear`, `github`, …)

## Symptom

```text
FailoverError: FallbackSummaryError: All models failed (2):
openai/gpt-5.5: plugin/list timed out (timeout) |
openai/gpt-5.4: plugin/list timed out (timeout)
<- FailoverError: plugin/list timed out: code=CODEX_APP_SERVER_LOCAL_REQUEST_CANCELLED
<- CodexAppServerLocalRequestCancellationError
```

Per attempt (gateway log): `wrote context-engine thread binding` → ~15–25 s → `model call diagnostic ended with error` → `one-shot cleanup retired shared client`; the spawned app-server child sits **idle at 0% CPU** the whole time. `openclaw doctor` also times out (rc=124), plausibly on the same path.

Not fixed by: gateway restarts, bumping the vendored Codex CLI 0.144.1 → 0.144.4, or updating OpenClaw across 300+ upstream commits (three heads tested). First occurrence in our logs: 2026-07-12 — predates all of those, so it is not a recent OpenClaw regression; it tracks a Codex-side plugin API change (see drift below).

## Codex app-server exonerated (direct stdio JSON-RPC, same binary + spawn args)

Spawning OpenClaw's exact command (`.../node_modules/@openai/codex-darwin-arm64/.../codex app-server --listen stdio:// -c cli_auth_credentials_store=ephemeral`) and sending `initialize` then:

| request | result |
|---|---|
| `plugin/list {"cwds":[]}` | OK, 0.5–0.8 s, 10 marketplaces |
| `plugin/list {"cwds":[],"marketplaceKinds":["workspace-directory"]}` | OK, 0.6 s |
| `plugin/list {"cwds":["<workspace>"]}` | OK, 0.8 s |

So the timeout is local to OpenClaw's request plumbing/budgeting in the activation path, not Codex latency.

## Two concrete API drifts under the activation path

1. **Curated marketplace name.** `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts` hardcodes `CODEX_PLUGINS_MARKETPLACE_NAME = "openai-curated"`, and configs written earlier use the same name — but current Codex (0.143+, remote plugins by default) exposes **no `openai-curated` marketplace**. Live `plugin/list` marketplaces: `openai-bundled`, `openai-curated-remote`, `openai-primary-runtime`, `openai-codex`, plus local/community entries.
2. **`plugin/install` contract.** Direct RPC with the old shape returns instantly:
   ```text
   plugin/install: ERROR 'plugin/install requires exactly one of marketplacePath or remoteMarketplaceName' (0.6 s)
   ```
   so activation-time installs against `marketplaceName` can no longer succeed.

A missing marketplace "should" surface as a clean `marketplace_missing` activation failure; instead the turn stalls until the request deadline and the surfaced error is the misleading `plugin/list timed out`, which masks the real problem — that's arguably a second bug (error shadowing in the activation/diagnostics chain).

## Workaround (confirmed)

```json
{ "plugins": { "entries": { "codex": { "config": { "codexPlugins": { "enabled": false } } } } } }
```

→ restart gateway → turns succeed immediately. (Same mitigation the reporter of #91352 landed on; that issue's inventory-refresh failure looks like a sibling symptom of this drift family. #94203 / #104188 added the remote/workspace marketplace support that the curated path predates.)

## Suggested direction

- Resolve the curated marketplace against the live `plugin/list` response (accept `openai-curated-remote`, keep `openai-curated` for older CLIs) rather than a single hardcoded name, and use `remoteMarketplaceName` in `plugin/install` for remote marketplaces.
- Fail activation fast and non-fatally (diagnostic, not turn-blocking) when the configured marketplace is absent, so a config/CLI drift can never take out 100% of turns.

Happy to contribute a PR for either half if useful — the reproduction harness (direct stdio RPC scripts + timings) is ready to share.
